events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. A one-tooth timing mark offset is a zero-tolerance defect — OEM timing specs require exact mark alignment at TDC, so any tooth-offset shifts valve events relative to piston position and risks interference damage or major driveability faults. Decision: **fail**, risk **high**, requiring re-indexing of the cam/crank sprocket before the timing cover is closed out or the engine is started.
